what: What’s a GEDI L1B waveform, and why bother?
title: GEDI L1B waveforms: from raw returns to height above ground
text: GEDI L2A gives summary elevation metrics per shot — ground elevation, relative height quantiles (RH0 RH100), canopy energy totals. ’s users want time. L1B step : one full waveform per shot, capturing lidar return amplitude function elevation. L1B waveform already smoothed Gaussian width 2 ns remove ADC sampling artefacts (Hofton & Blair, 2019, Section 3.2.3); raw telemetered signal (lives L1A, publicly distributed). Everything L2A derived L1B peak detection, additional smoothing, mode fitting. Working directly L1B worth want : See canopy structure, just metrics derived Detect features L2A’s algorithms didn’t capture (understory layers, near-ground returns steep terrain, water-surface returns) Build metrics validate L2A’s ground detection L1B signal vignette walks L1B waveforms height--detected-ground plots joining L1B L2A shot_number using L2A’s elev_lowestmode per-shot zero reference.

title: GEDI L1B waveforms: from raw returns to height above ground
text: ’ll work small bbox Pacific Northwest coast — coastal redwoods, 50–100 m tall — difference “height ellipsoid”, “height SRTM”, “height detected ground” dramatic enough visually obvious.

title: GEDI L1B waveforms: from raw returns to height above ground
text: Search cheap, issue twice — per product — sl_read shared bbox. Restrict single granule per product keep vignette snappy; remove [1L, ] full pipeline. l1b carries waveform (rxwaveform, already Gaussian-smoothed L1B processing stage) structural metadata (elevation_bin0, elevation_lastbin, rx_sample_count), plus noise statistics, DEM references, per-shot metadata. l2a carries processed ground elevation (elev_lowestmode), quality flags (quality_flag, sensitivity, degrade_flag), relative-height quantiles (rh0 rh100).
code: g_l1b <- sl_search(bbox, product = "L1B",                    date_start = dates$start, date_end = dates$end) #> ℹ Searching CMR for GEDI L1B granules #> ✔ Searching CMR for GEDI L1B granules [551ms] #>  #> ✔ Found 1 GEDI L1B granule. g_l2a <- sl_search(bbox, product = "L2A",                    date_start = dates$start, date_end = dates$end) #> ℹ Searching CMR for GEDI L2A granules #> ✔ Searching CMR for GEDI L2A granules [249ms] #>  #> ✔ Found 1 GEDI L2A granule.  l1b <- sl_read(g_l1b[1L, ], bbox = bbox) #> ℹ Reading L1B from 1 granule #> ✔ Read 161 footprints from 6 beams.✔ Reading L1B from 1 granule [19.1s] l2a <- sl_read(g_l2a[1L, ], bbox = bbox) #> ℹ Reading L2A from 1 granule #> ✔ Read 159 footprints from 6 beams.✔ Reading L2A from 1 granule [15.2s]

what: Join L1B with L2A ground elevations
title: GEDI L1B waveforms: from raw returns to height above ground
text: shot_number shared key. Two granules orbit bbox cover shots, plain inner join gives waveforms detected ground elevations side side. Quality filtering matters: L1B happily hand waveforms shots L2A flagged low-sensitivity (e.g. dense cloud cover, high solar contamination). Keep shots L2A trusts ground detection.
code: l1b_joined <- l1b |>   inner_join(     l2a |> select(shot_number, elev_lowestmode, quality_flag, sensitivity,                   rh25, rh50, rh75, rh95, rh100),     by = "shot_number"   ) |>   filter(     quality_flag == 1L,  # L2A quality gate     sensitivity > 0.9,   # only well-penetrated shots     solar_elevation < 0  # night shots for cleaner returns   )  nrow(l1b_joined) #> [1] 157

what: Extract waveforms, then re-reference to ground
title: GEDI L1B waveforms: from raw returns to height above ground
text: sl_extract_waveforms knows subtract DEM references, doesn’t know L2A ground. Extract ellipsoidal space (default) subtract elev_lowestmode per shot post-hoc. height_above_ground = 0 now detected ground return; positive canopy, negative noise samples ground RX window continues. used normalise_amplitude = "snr" , subtracts per-shot noise mean divides per-shot noise standard deviation. puts every shot common scale regardless digitiser channel captured (GEDI’s channels different baseline pedestals; without normalisation, waveforms different beams cluster different baselines). alternative "noise" subtracts mean skips division, leaving amplitude baseline-subtracted ADC counts; "raw" (default) returns waveform -. SNR units convenient overlaying multiple shots noise floor sits zero unit variance across shots, regardless beam channel. value 4 mean “4 standard deviations noise”, provides intuitive scale distinguishing signal background.
code: wf <- sl_extract_waveforms(   l1b_joined,   height_ref = "ellipsoid",    normalise_amplitude = "snr"   # noise-normalised so beams/channels compare )  # per-sample ground reference, looked up by shot ground_by_sample <- l1b_joined$elev_lowestmode[   match(wf$shot_number, l1b_joined$shot_number) ]  wf$height_above_ground <- wf$elevation - ground_by_sample

title: GEDI L1B waveforms: from raw returns to height above ground
text: stacking hundreds shots composite, ’s worth unpacking single waveform actually contains. Pick shot tall canopy (rh95 50 m) plot amplitude vs height, annotated L2A-derived structural metrics.  Things notice: strong peak 0 m L2A detected ground — ’s height_above_ground = 0 lines . ground-referencing peak sat ellipsoidal elevation; shifted zero . broader peak around rh95 approximately canopy top. rh95 defined 95 % waveform energy (cumulative ground ) returned height — can see waveform fall sharply just . rh50 marks median return energy, usually mid-canopy. rh50 rh95 foliage signal lives. Anything rh95 either sparse emergent canopy, atmospheric return, noise — typically low amplitude.
code: tall_shot <- l1b_joined |>   arrange(desc(rh95)) |>   slice(5L) |>  # 5th tallest rh95 (avoids possible outliers at the very top)   pull(shot_number)  wf_one  <- wf |> filter(shot_number == tall_shot) meta_one <- l1b_joined |> filter(shot_number == tall_shot)  ggplot(wf_one, aes(amplitude, height_above_ground)) +   geom_path(colour = "#1d743eff", linewidth = 0.5) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0,             colour = "#d48282",              linewidth = 0.4) +   geom_hline(yintercept = meta_one$rh50, colour = "#8782d4",              linewidth = 0.3, linetype = "dashed") +   geom_hline(yintercept = meta_one$rh95, colour = "#8782d4",              linewidth = 0.3, linetype = "dashed") +   annotate("text", x = Inf, y = 0,            label = "ground", hjust = 1.1, vjust = -0.4,            colour = "#d48282", size = 3) +   annotate("text", x = Inf, y = meta_one$rh50,            label = "rh50", hjust = 1.1, vjust = -0.4,            colour = "#8782d4", size = 3) +   annotate("text", x = Inf, y = meta_one$rh95,            label = "rh95 (canopy top)", hjust = 1.1, vjust = -0.4,            colour = "#8782d4", size = 3) +   coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-10, 120)) +   labs(     x = "Amplitude (noise σ units)",     y = "Height above detected ground (m)",     title = sprintf("Single GEDI L1B waveform — rh95 = %.0f m",                     meta_one$rh95)   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: Comparing two shots: short vs tall
title: GEDI L1B waveforms: from raw returns to height above ground
text: machinery different shot looks different. Pick one shot low rh95 compare tall one .  short shot (open ground low vegetation) dominated single ground peak almost energy 10 metres. tall shot clear canopy structure: ground return zero plus broad canopy-top return 50-90 m.
code: # tall_shot is already defined from the anatomy section above short_shot <- l1b_joined |>   filter(rh95 < 15) |> slice(1L) |> pull(shot_number)  wf_pair <- wf |>   filter(shot_number %in% c(short_shot, tall_shot)) |>   left_join(     l1b_joined |> select(shot_number, rh50, rh95),     by = "shot_number"   ) |>   mutate(     label = sprintf("shot %s\nrh50 = %.0f m, rh95 = %.0f m",                     shot_number, rh50, rh95)   )  ggplot(wf_pair, aes(amplitude, height_above_ground)) +   geom_path(colour = "#1d743eff", linewidth = 0.5) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "#d48282", linewidth = 0.3) +   facet_wrap(~ label) +   coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-10, 120)) +   labs(     x = "Amplitude (noise σ units)",     y = "Height above detected ground (m)"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: GEDI L1B waveforms: from raw returns to height above ground
text: Now know one waveform looks like, let’s stack . overlay amplitude quantiles height composite reads structural profile rather spaghetti.  median line “typical” waveform bbox: sharp peak ground, secondary peak near median canopy height, decaying returns . 10-90 % ribbon shows shot--shot variation — narrow bands mean consistent structure across bbox, wide bands mean heterogeneous canopy (edge shots, gaps, short vs tall mixed).
code: wf_quantiles <- wf |>   filter(height_above_ground >= 0, height_above_ground <= 200) |>   mutate(h_bin = round(height_above_ground)) |>   group_by(h_bin) |>   summarise(     q10 = quantile(amplitude, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),     q50 = median(amplitude,         na.rm = TRUE),     q90 = quantile(amplitude, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot() +   geom_ribbon(     data = wf_quantiles,     aes(xmin = q10, xmax = q90, y = h_bin),     fill = "#24964fff", alpha = 0.1, colour= "#888888ff", linewidth = 0.4   ) +   geom_path(     data = wf,     aes(amplitude, height_above_ground, group = shot_number), colour = "#24964fff", linewidth = 0.1, alpha=0.6   ) +      geom_path(     data = wf_quantiles,     aes(q50, h_bin),     colour = "#175e32ff", linewidth = 0.8   ) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "#d48282", linewidth = 0.8) +   coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 120)) +   scale_x_continuous(trans = "pseudo_log") +   labs(     x = "Amplitude (noise σ units)",     y = "Height above detected ground (m)",     title    = "L1B waveforms over PNW coastal redwood",     subtitle = "Individual shots (green paths), 10-90% amplitude range (ribbon), median (line)"   ) +   theme_minimal() #> Don't know how to automatically pick scale for object of type <integer64>. #> Defaulting to continuous.

what: Cross-check: compute your own RH metrics
title: GEDI L1B waveforms: from raw returns to height above ground
text: RHN elevation N% cumulative waveform energy (ground ) returned. waveforms zeroed ground, can compute -place compare L2A.  points hug 1:1 line, simple-minded cumulative-energy RH95 agrees L2A’s principled version. Disagreements identify shots L2A’s noise thresholding, smoothing, algorithm selection (selected_algorithm) produces meaningfully different answer naive cumsum. disagreements L2A right thing, way around ;).
code: # cumulative energy from ground up, per shot rh_custom <- wf |>   filter(height_above_ground >= 0) |>       # ignore below-ground noise   group_by(shot_number) |>   arrange(height_above_ground, .by_group = TRUE) |>   mutate(cum_energy = cumsum(amplitude) / sum(amplitude)) |>   summarise(     rh50_ours = approx(cum_energy, height_above_ground, xout = 0.50)$y,     rh95_ours = approx(cum_energy, height_above_ground, xout = 0.95)$y,     .groups = "drop"   ) #> Warning: There were 4 warnings in `summarise()`. #> The first warning was: #> ℹ In argument: `rh50_ours = approx(cum_energy, height_above_ground, xout = #>   0.5)$y`. #> ℹ In group 100: `shot_number = 90690600300513532`. #> Caused by warning in `regularize.values()`: #> ! collapsing to unique 'x' values #> ℹ Run `dplyr::last_dplyr_warnings()` to see the 3 remaining warnings.  comparison <- l1b_joined |>   select(shot_number, rh50, rh95) |>   inner_join(rh_custom, by = "shot_number")  ggplot(comparison, aes(rh95, rh95_ours)) +   geom_abline(colour = "#d48282") +   geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +   coord_equal() +   labs(x = "L2A rh95 (m)", y = "Custom rh95 (m)")

title: GEDI L1B waveforms: from raw returns to height above ground
text: Algorithm selection. L2A ships six algorithm variants per shot picks one via selected_algorithm. cumulative-energy RH uses none machinery. Ground detection scratch. used L2A’s elev_lowestmode rather detecting ground . naive ground detection dense canopy steep terrain tricky likely worse L2A’s algorithm; need ground peak, read L2A. Noise mean independently corrected. Per ATBD (Section 3.2.1), “corrections currently applied mean noise level; value telemetered datapacket recorded.” column name noise_mean_corrected refers noise mean corrected (smoothed) waveform, adjusted mean. noise_stddev_corrected receives active correction (odd-even sampler offsets, Section 3.2.2). distinction immaterial subtraction SNR normalisation perform : mean standard deviation measured smoothed waveform analyse, units match.

title: GEDI L1B waveforms: from raw returns to height above ground
text: Hofton, M. . & Blair, J. B. (2019). Algorithm Theoretical Basis Document GEDI Transmit Receive Waveform Processing L1 L2 Products, v1.0. NASA Goddard Space Flight Center. Covers L1B waveform smoothing, noise correction, L2A/L2B algorithm chain referenced throughout vignette.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Hugh Graham. Author, maintainer. belian.earth. Copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Graham H (2026). spacelaser: Cloud-Optimized Partial Reading GEDI ICESat-2 HDF5 Data. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://github.com/belian-earth/spacelaser.
code: @Manual{,   title = {spacelaser: Cloud-Optimized Partial Reading of GEDI and ICESat-2 HDF5 Data},   author = {Hugh Graham},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/belian-earth/spacelaser}, }

what: Cloud-Optimized Partial Reading of GEDI and ICESat-2 HDF5 Data
title: Cloud-Optimized Partial Reading of GEDI and ICESat-2 HDF5 Data
text: Fast Cloud-optimized partial reading GEDI ICESat-2 HDF5 data R. bytes needed requested spatial/temporal subset fetched HTTP, avoiding multi-gigabyte downloads.

title: Cloud-Optimized Partial Reading of GEDI and ICESat-2 HDF5 Data
text: Requires Rust toolchain (cargo + rustc).
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("belian-earth/spacelaser")

title: Cloud-Optimized Partial Reading of GEDI and ICESat-2 HDF5 Data
text: reads go NASA Earthdata, requires free account. Register https://urs.earthdata.nasa.gov/. Credentials can supplied following ways: Environment variables — set EARTHDATA_USERNAME EARTHDATA_PASSWORD. Convenient CI shell sessions. .netrc file — add entry urs.earthdata.nasa.gov ~/.netrc (_netrc Windows). spacelaser read directly. earthdatalogin — simplest option don’t already netrc set : writes netrc interoperable R Earthdata tools.
code: # install.packages("earthdatalogin") earthdatalogin::edl_netrc()

title: Cloud-Optimized Partial Reading of GEDI and ICESat-2 HDF5 Data
code: library(spacelaser)  bbox <- sl_bbox(-124.04, 41.39, -124.01, 41.42)  granules <- sl_search(   bbox,   product = "L2A",   date_start = "2022-01-01",   date_end = "2023-01-01" ) #> ℹ Searching CMR for GEDI L2A granules #> ✔ Searching CMR for GEDI L2A granules [2.9s] #>  #> ✔ Found 9 GEDI L2A granules. gedi2a <- sl_read(granules) #> ℹ Reading L2A from 9 granules #> ✔ Read 647 footprints from 20 beams.✔ Reading L2A from 9 granules [1m 2.3s]  gedi2a #> # A tibble: 647 × 121 #>    beam     shot_number time                lat_lowestmode lon_lowestmode #>    <chr>        <int64> <dttm>                       <dbl>          <dbl> #>  1 BEAM1000       2.e17 2022-11-25 06:16:52           41.4          -124. #>  2 BEAM1000       2.e17 2022-11-25 06:16:52           41.4          -124. #>  3 BEAM1000       2.e17 2022-11-25 06:16:52           41.4          -124. #>  4 BEAM1000       2.e17 2022-11-25 06:16:52           41.4          -124. #>  5 BEAM1000       2.e17 2022-11-25 06:16:53           41.4          -124. #>  6 BEAM1011       2.e17 2022-11-25 06:16:52           41.4          -124. #>  7 BEAM1011       2.e17 2022-11-25 06:16:52           41.4          -124. #>  8 BEAM1011       2.e17 2022-11-25 06:16:52           41.4          -124. #>  9 BEAM1011       2.e17 2022-11-25 06:16:52           41.4          -124. #> 10 BEAM1011       2.e17 2022-11-25 06:16:52           41.4          -124. #> # ℹ 637 more rows #> # ℹ 116 more variables: degrade_flag <int>, quality_flag <int>, #> #   sensitivity <dbl>, solar_elevation <dbl>, elev_lowestmode <dbl>, #> #   elev_highestreturn <dbl>, energy_total <dbl>, num_detectedmodes <int>, #> #   rh0 <dbl>, rh1 <dbl>, rh2 <dbl>, rh3 <dbl>, rh4 <dbl>, rh5 <dbl>, #> #   rh6 <dbl>, rh7 <dbl>, rh8 <dbl>, rh9 <dbl>, rh10 <dbl>, rh11 <dbl>, #> #   rh12 <dbl>, rh13 <dbl>, rh14 <dbl>, rh15 <dbl>, rh16 <dbl>, rh17 <dbl>, …  g <- gedi2a[gedi2a$quality_flag == 1, ]  plot(   g$geometry,   pch = 21,   cex = 1.5,   bg = hcl.colors(100, "Viridis", alpha = 0.7)[     findInterval(g$rh98, seq(0, 100), all.inside = TRUE)   ] )

title: Cloud-Optimized Partial Reading of GEDI and ICESat-2 HDF5 Data
text: sl_columns() lists product offers. 12 GEDI ICESat-2 products supported spacelaser use two verbs (sl_search() → sl_read()); product string column names change.
code: # ICESat-2 photon-level data — full column inventory sl_columns("ATL03", set = "all") #>                    lat_ph                    lon_ph                      h_ph  #>          "heights/lat_ph"          "heights/lon_ph"            "heights/h_ph"  #>                delta_time            signal_conf_ph            dist_ph_across  #>      "heights/delta_time"  "heights/signal_conf_ph"  "heights/dist_ph_across"  #>             dist_ph_along            pce_mframe_cnt             ph_id_channel  #>   "heights/dist_ph_along"  "heights/pce_mframe_cnt"   "heights/ph_id_channel"  #>               ph_id_count               ph_id_pulse                quality_ph  #>     "heights/ph_id_count"     "heights/ph_id_pulse"      "heights/quality_ph"  #>           signal_class_ph                 weight_ph  #> "heights/signal_class_ph"       "heights/weight_ph"

title: Cloud-Optimized Partial Reading of GEDI and ICESat-2 HDF5 Data
text: standard R workflow GEDI / ICESat-2 data download whole HDF5 granules, filter locally. typical spatial subset query wastes minutes gigabytes — file ’re filtering usually orders magnitude larger answer actually want. Spacelaser sends HTTP range requests remote files returns just rows fall inside bounding box, local caching needed. representative Mondah Forest workload (11 GEDI L2A granules, two years coverage, 1,376 matching shots) spacelaser completes ~60 s versus ~1,170 s full-granule download + hdf5r read — around 19× quicker 112 shared columns, bit--bit identical output. See benchmarks/ methodology comparisons partial-read pre-indexed approaches.

title: Cloud-Optimized Partial Reading of GEDI and ICESat-2 HDF5 Data
text: spacelaser Rust reimplementation partial-HDF5 reading approach pioneered h5coro (NASA SlideRule Earth). core idea : targeted HTTP range requests cloud-hosted HDF5 granules rather downloading whole files. package brings idea R GEDI/ICESat-2-specific API ground-Rust parser.

title: Cloud-Optimized Partial Reading of GEDI and ICESat-2 HDF5 Data
text: GEDI: NASA GEDI Science Team, distributed LP DAAC ORNL DAAC ICESat-2: NASA ATLAS Instrument Science Teams, distributed NSIDC DAAC

what: Create a bounding box for spatial queries — sl_bbox
title: Create a bounding box for spatial queries — sl_bbox
text: Wraps four corner coordinates sl_bbox vector used sl_search() sl_read(). main value -front validation: arguments checked correct ordering (xmin < xmax, ymin < ymax) coordinates falling within WGS84 bounds (latitude [-90, 90], longitude [-180, 180]), mistakes surface rather silent empty search failed HTTP request.

title: Create a bounding box for spatial queries — sl_bbox
code: sl_bbox(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax)

title: Create a bounding box for spatial queries — sl_bbox
text: xmin Minimum longitude (western boundary). ymin Minimum latitude (southern boundary). xmax Maximum longitude (eastern boundary). ymax Maximum latitude (northern boundary).

title: Create a bounding box for spatial queries — sl_bbox
text: named double vector class sl_bbox.

title: Create a bounding box for spatial queries — sl_bbox
code: # Construct a bounding box over a Pacific Northwest forest site. sl_bbox(-124.04, 41.39, -124.01, 41.42) #> <sl_bbox>: (-124.0400, 41.3900) - (-124.0100, 41.4200)  # Validation catches common mistakes before they reach the search # or reader. Wrap in try() so the example chunk keeps running. try(sl_bbox(-124.01, 41.39, -124.04, 41.42))  # xmin >= xmax #> Error in sl_bbox(-124.01, 41.39, -124.04, 41.42) :  #>   `xmin` must be less than `xmax`. try(sl_bbox(0, -100, 1, 1))                   # latitude out of range #> Error in sl_bbox(0, -100, 1, 1) :  #>   Latitude values must be between -90 and 90.

what: List available columns for a GEDI or ICESat-2 product — sl_columns
title: List available columns for a GEDI or ICESat-2 product — sl_columns
text: Returns named character vector columns given product. Names short user-facing column names (used columns argument sl_read()). Values full HDF5 dataset paths.

title: List available columns for a GEDI or ICESat-2 product — sl_columns
code: sl_columns(   product = c("L2A", "L2B", "L4A", "L4C", "L1B", "ATL03", "ATL06", "ATL07", "ATL08",     "ATL10", "ATL13", "ATL24"),   set = c("all", "default") )

title: List available columns for a GEDI or ICESat-2 product — sl_columns
text: product Character. One : GEDI: "L1B", "L2A", "L2B", "L4A", "L4C" ICESat-2: "ATL03", "ATL06", "ATL07", "ATL08", "ATL10", "ATL13", "ATL24" set Character. column set return: "" (default): every column registry, including geophysical corrections, instrument details, pool columns. "default": curated subset commonly useful science variables, quality flags, context columns. sl_read() returns columns specified.

title: List available columns for a GEDI or ICESat-2 product — sl_columns
text: named character vector.

what: Extract L1B waveforms to long form with elevation profile — sl_extract_waveforms
title: Extract L1B waveforms to long form with elevation profile — sl_extract_waveforms
text: Converts GEDI L1B received-waveform list columns long-form data frame one row per waveform sample, sample assigned elevation linearly interpolating elevation_bin0 (waveform top) elevation_lastbin (waveform bottom). elevation reference configurable via height_ref amplitude normalisation via normalise_amplitude.

title: Extract L1B waveforms to long form with elevation profile — sl_extract_waveforms
code: sl_extract_waveforms(   x,   height_ref = c("ellipsoid", "geoid", "tandemx", "srtm"),   normalise_amplitude = c("raw", "noise", "snr") )

title: Extract L1B waveforms to long form with elevation profile — sl_extract_waveforms
text: x data frame sl_read() containing GEDI L1B data. Must include shot_number, elevation_bin0, elevation_lastbin, rx_sample_count, rxwaveform (list column). beam present, carried output. Additional columns required non-default height_ref normalise_amplitude values — see description mode . height_ref Reference surface returned elevation column: "ellipsoid" (default) WGS-84 ellipsoidal elevation, exactly reported L1B. extra columns needed. "geoid" Height EGM2008 geoid (mean sea level): elevation - geoid per shot. Requires geoid. "tandemx" Height TanDEM-X 90 m DEM footprint: elevation - digital_elevation_model per shot. rough proxy height ground L2A ground return available. Requires digital_elevation_model. "srtm" "tandemx", SRTM DEM. Requires digital_elevation_model_srtm. normalise_amplitude Baseline correction returned amplitude column. Raw GEDI waveform samples carry per-shot pedestal differs digitiser channel, raw amplitudes different shots / beams / channels directly comparable. "raw" (default) Sample values straight rxwaveform. extra columns needed. "noise" Baseline-subtracted amplitude: rxwaveform - noise_mean_corrected per shot, every shot's free-atmosphere noise floor sits zero. Requires noise_mean_corrected. "snr" Noise-normalised amplitude: (rxwaveform - noise_mean_corrected) / noise_stddev_corrected per shot, expressed units per-shot noise standard deviation. Useful peak-detection. Requires noise_mean_corrected noise_stddev_corrected.

title: Extract L1B waveforms to long form with elevation profile — sl_extract_waveforms
text: data frame one row per waveform sample: shot_number Shot identifier (repeated per sample). beam Beam name, present input. elevation Sample elevation metres, referenced surface chosen height_ref. amplitude Waveform return amplitude, adjusted according normalise_amplitude.

title: Extract L1B waveforms to long form with elevation profile — sl_extract_waveforms
text: per-sample elevation computed elevation[] = elevation_bin0 - * (elevation_bin0 - elevation_lastbin) / rx_sample_count 1..rx_sample_count, placing sample 1 one step elevation_bin0 sample rx_sample_count elevation_lastbin (chewie convention). height_ref argument subtracts corresponding per-shot reference (geoid, DEM, nothing) every sample. L1B default column set includes rxwaveform, structural dependency columns (elevation_bin0, elevation_lastbin, rx_sample_count, shot_number), height references (geoid, digital_elevation_model, digital_elevation_model_srtm), noise statistics (noise_mean_corrected, noise_stddev_corrected). Every height_ref normalise_amplitude mode therefore works box plain sl_read(granules). narrowing read columns = c(...), request columns needed chosen modes. Shots NA reference elevations, NA noise statistics (requested), zero rx_sample_count dropped silently.

title: Extract L1B waveforms to long form with elevation profile — sl_extract_waveforms
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() bbox <- sl_bbox(-124.04, 41.39, -124.01, 41.42) granules <- sl_search(bbox, product = "L1B",                       date_start = "2020-06-01",                       date_end   = "2020-06-30") d <- sl_read(granules[1L, ], bbox = bbox)  # Absolute ellipsoidal elevations, raw amplitudes (defaults). wf <- sl_extract_waveforms(d)  # Height above the TanDEM-X DEM with baseline-subtracted amplitude — # waveforms from different beams / channels line up at zero. wf_agl <- sl_extract_waveforms(   d,   height_ref         = "tandemx",   normalise_amplitude = "noise" )  # SNR units: amplitudes in multiples of per-shot noise σ. wf_snr <- sl_extract_waveforms(d, normalise_amplitude = "snr") }

what: List groups in a remote HDF5 file — sl_hdf5_groups
title: List groups in a remote HDF5 file — sl_hdf5_groups
text: Low-level helper exploring internal structure remote HDF5 file. Useful discovering available datasets, beams, subgroups working outside curated sl_columns() registry. Earthdata credentials required (see sl_search() setup).

title: List groups in a remote HDF5 file — sl_hdf5_groups
code: sl_hdf5_groups(url, path = "/")

title: List groups in a remote HDF5 file — sl_hdf5_groups
text: url Character. HTTPS URL HDF5 file. path Character. HDF5 group path list (default: "/").

title: List groups in a remote HDF5 file — sl_hdf5_groups
text: character vector group/dataset names.

title: List groups in a remote HDF5 file — sl_hdf5_groups
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() url <- paste0(   "https://data.lpdaac.earthdatacloud.nasa.gov/lp-prod-protected/",   "GEDI02_A.002/GEDI02_A_2020009130403_O06095_03_T02944_02_003_01_V002/",   "GEDI02_A_2020009130403_O06095_03_T02944_02_003_01_V002.h5" ) sl_hdf5_groups(url) sl_hdf5_groups(url, path = "BEAM0000/geolocation") }

what: Read a single dataset from a remote HDF5 file — sl_hdf5_read
title: Read a single dataset from a remote HDF5 file — sl_hdf5_read
text: Low-level helper reads arbitrary HDF5 dataset full path returns appropriately-typed R vector. Useful pulling individual fields covered curated registry, inspecting dataset requesting via sl_read(). Earthdata credentials required (see sl_search() setup).

title: Read a single dataset from a remote HDF5 file — sl_hdf5_read
code: sl_hdf5_read(url, dataset)

title: Read a single dataset from a remote HDF5 file — sl_hdf5_read
text: url Character. HTTPS URL HDF5 file. dataset Character. Full HDF5 path dataset (e.g., "/BEAM0101/lat_lowestmode").

title: Read a single dataset from a remote HDF5 file — sl_hdf5_read
text: R vector — numeric, integer, raw, depending underlying HDF5 datatype.

title: Read a single dataset from a remote HDF5 file — sl_hdf5_read
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() url <- paste0(   "https://data.lpdaac.earthdatacloud.nasa.gov/lp-prod-protected/",   "GEDI02_A.002/GEDI02_A_2020009130403_O06095_03_T02944_02_003_01_V002/",   "GEDI02_A_2020009130403_O06095_03_T02944_02_003_01_V002.h5" ) lat <- sl_hdf5_read(url, "BEAM0000/lat_lowestmode") str(lat) }

title: Read satellite lidar data — sl_read
text: sl_read() S3 generic reads GEDI ICESat-2 data remote HDF5 files using HTTP range requests. chunks intersecting bounding box fetched; full-file download. dispatches type first argument:

title: Read satellite lidar data — sl_read
code: sl_read(x, bbox, ...)  # S3 method for class 'sl_gedi_search' sl_read(x, bbox = NULL, columns = NULL, convert_time = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'sl_icesat2_search' sl_read(x, bbox = NULL, columns = NULL, convert_time = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'character' sl_read(x, bbox, ..., product = NULL)

title: Read satellite lidar data — sl_read
text: x sl_gedi_search, sl_icesat2_search, character vector HDF5 URLs. bbox sl_bbox numeric c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax). Required x character vector. Optional x search result: defaults bbox search performed . supplied, must fully contained within search bbox; supplying wider bbox error avoid silently missing data outside original search. ... Reserved method-specific arguments forwarding. columns Character vector column names read (short names sl_columns()). Latitude longitude always included automatically. NULL (default) reads curated default column set product (see sl_columns() set = "default"). Pass names(sl_columns(product)) read available columns. convert_time Logical. TRUE (default), raw delta_time column (seconds since GEDI / ICESat-2 reference epoch 2018-01-01 00:00:00 UTC) converted POSIXct column named time. Set FALSE keep delta_time raw numeric seconds-since-epoch value, e.g. need compare file-level epoch exactly, want avoid POSIXct conversion overhead large photon-level reads, want preserve original HDF5 column name. product Character. Product level (e.g., "L2A", "ATL08"). Required x character vector product inferred file name.

title: Read satellite lidar data — sl_read
text: data frame one row per footprint (GEDI) segment/photon (ICESat-2). Columns depend product columns argument. Fill-value sentinels (-9999, 3.4e38, etc.) automatically replaced NA.

title: Read satellite lidar data — sl_read
text: sl_gedi_search sl_icesat2_search object (sl_search()): reads granules search result, combining rows single data frame. search bbox used default; explicit bbox may supplied subset must contained within . character vector URLs: auto-detects sensor product file name, uses explicit product argument. beams (GEDI) ground tracks (ICESat-2) always read; returned data frame includes beam (GEDI) track (ICESat-2) identifier column post-hoc filtering dplyr::filter() base subsetting.

title: Read satellite lidar data — sl_read
text: columns = NULL, curated default set returned product. Use sl_columns(product, set = "default") see columns included. defaults designed cover primary science variables, key quality flags, basic context without surprises. Use sl_columns(product, set = "") discover everything available.

title: Read satellite lidar data — sl_read
text: GEDI L1B: default set includes rxwaveform, list column (one numeric vector per shot containing received waveform). Use sl_extract_waveforms() expand long-form data frame per-sample elevations. transmitted waveform (txwaveform) available via explicit request included defaults. L1B reads slower products waveform data requires targeted chunk reads pool dataset. GEDI L2A: rh column 2D dataset [N, 101] expands 101 columns (rh0 rh100), representing relative height percentiles metres. included default set. GEDI L2B: default set includes cover_z, pai_z, pavd_z, 2D datasets [N, 30] representing canopy vertical profiles 5 m height bins. expands 30 columns (e.g. cover_z0 cover_z29), adding 90 columns output. pgap_theta_z variable-length list column (similar L1B waveforms) included defaults; request explicitly needed. GEDI L2B rh100: Stored centimetres HDF5 file; automatically converted metres consistency L2A. GEDI L4A: agbd column -ground biomass density Mg/ha. Prediction intervals (agbd_pi_lower, agbd_pi_upper) standard error (agbd_se) included defaults. GEDI L4C: Waveform Structural Complexity Index. wsci column headline metric, prediction intervals (wsci_pi_lower, wsci_pi_upper) decomposed XY/Z components (wsci_xy, wsci_z) defaults. worldcover_class provides ESA WorldCover land-cover class. ICESat-2 ATL03: Photon-level data. single granule can contain millions photons. reader uses ATL03's segment-level spatial index (geolocation/reference_photon_lat etc.) filter segment rate reading photon-level columns, spatial subsets stay fast even large bboxes. signal_conf_ph 2D column [N, 5] (5 surface types: land, ocean, sea ice, land ice, inland water) expands 5 columns. ICESat-2 ATL06: Land ice elevation segments. default set includes fit statistics (n_fit_photons, h_robust_sprd, snr) reference DEM height (dem_h). Tidal geophysical corrections available via sl_columns("ATL06") defaults. ICESat-2 ATL07: Sea-ice height segments. Defaults include segment height + confidence + quality, photon rate, AMSR2 ice concentration, atmospheric flags. Geolocation parameters (solar_*, sigma_h) finer geophysical corrections live sea_ice_segments/{geolocation,geophysical,stats}/ available via sl_columns("ATL07", set = ""). ICESat-2 ATL08: default set includes canopy_h_metrics, 2D dataset [N, 18] canopy height percentiles (P10 P95) expands 18 columns. Terrain slope, photon counts, land cover also included. *_abs (absolute height) variants secondary canopy / terrain metrics registry defaults. ICESat-2 ATL10: Sea-ice freeboard. Defaults include beam_fb_height (per-beam freeboard) plus quality, confidence, underlying ATL07 height-segment context. ICESat-2 ATL13: Inland water surface heights. Defaults include water-surface height + standard deviation, significant wave height, water depth, segment provenance (inland_water_body_*), quality flags. Geometry uses segment centroid (segment_lat, segment_lon). ICESat-2 ATL24: Near-shore bathymetry, photon-level. Defaults include orthometric / ellipsoidal / surface heights, photon class confidence, THU / TVU positional uncertainty pair. Geometry uses photon coordinates (lat_ph, lon_ph).

what: Search NASA CMR for GEDI or ICESat-2 granules — sl_search
title: Search NASA CMR for GEDI or ICESat-2 granules — sl_search
text: Searches NASA's Common Metadata Repository (CMR) satellite lidar granules overlap bounding box optional date range. sensor (GEDI ICESat-2) determined automatically product, since valid product strings overlap.

title: Search NASA CMR for GEDI or ICESat-2 granules — sl_search
code: sl_search(   bbox,   product = c("L2A", "L2B", "L4A", "L4C", "L1B", "ATL03", "ATL06", "ATL07", "ATL08",     "ATL10", "ATL13", "ATL24"),   date_start = NULL,   date_end = NULL )

title: Search NASA CMR for GEDI or ICESat-2 granules — sl_search
text: bbox sl_bbox object created sl_bbox(), numeric vector c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax). product Character. One : GEDI: "L1B", "L2A", "L2B", "L4A", "L4C" ICESat-2: "ATL03", "ATL06", "ATL07", "ATL08", "ATL10", "ATL13", "ATL24" date_start, date_end Either Date object character string strict "YYYY-MM-DD" format (e.g. "2020-06-01"). character forms (e.g. "01/06/2020", "June 1 2020", "2020-06") rejected informative error avoid ambiguity. POSIXct inputs accepted — format explicitly format(x, "%Y-%m-%d"). Bounds treated UTC inclusive end date: range "2020-06-01" "2020-06-30" covers every granule whose start time falls 30 days. date_start defaults mission start (2019-03-25 GEDI, 2018-10-14 ICESat-2). date_end defaults today.

title: Search NASA CMR for GEDI or ICESat-2 granules — sl_search
text: classed data frame (sl_gedi_search sl_icesat2_search) columns: id CMR granule identifier. time_start POSIXct start time granule. time_end POSIXct end time granule. url HTTPS data URL. geometry wk_wkt polygon granule swath footprint. returned object carries bbox product attributes sl_read() can dispatch without re-specifying .

title: Search NASA CMR for GEDI or ICESat-2 granules — sl_search
text: authentication needed search ; Earthdata credentials required reading data via sl_read(). CMR search filters bounding box server side. finer spatial filtering (e.g. irregular polygon), filter returned geometry column favourite spatial package.

title: Search NASA CMR for GEDI or ICESat-2 granules — sl_search
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() # GEDI L2A over a small Pacific Northwest forest bbox, summer 2020. granules <- sl_search(   sl_bbox(-124.04, 41.39, -124.01, 41.42),   product    = "L2A",   date_start = "2020-06-01",   date_end   = "2020-09-01" ) granules  # ICESat-2 ATL08 (land + canopy segments) over the same bbox, 2020. sl_search(   sl_bbox(-124.10, 41.36, -124.00, 41.45),   product    = "ATL08",   date_start = "2020-01-01",   date_end   = "2021-01-01" ) }

what: spacelaser: Fast Remote Reads of GEDI and ICESat-2 Lidar Data — spacelaser-package
title: spacelaser: Fast Remote Reads of GEDI and ICESat-2 Lidar Data — spacelaser-package
text: R bindings pure-Rust HDF5 parser (via extendr) fetches spatial subsets NASA GEDI ICESat-2 lidar granules HTTP range requests, avoiding multi-gigabyte downloads. Returns tibbles wk geometry columns, ready analysis. Supported products: GEDI L1B / L2A / L2B / L4A / L4C ICESat-2 ATL03 / ATL06 / ATL07 / ATL08 / ATL10 / ATL13 / ATL24.

title: spacelaser: Fast Remote Reads of GEDI and ICESat-2 Lidar Data — spacelaser-package
text: sl_search() — find granules via NASA CMR sl_bbox() — construct bounding box spatial queries

title: spacelaser: Fast Remote Reads of GEDI and ICESat-2 Lidar Data — spacelaser-package
text: sl_read() — pull spatial subsets remote HDF5 granules (dispatches sl_gedi_search / sl_icesat2_search objects character vector URLs)

title: spacelaser: Fast Remote Reads of GEDI and ICESat-2 Lidar Data — spacelaser-package
text: sl_columns() — inspect curated column registry given product; returns available column names, HDF5 paths, types, whether default read set

title: spacelaser: Fast Remote Reads of GEDI and ICESat-2 Lidar Data — spacelaser-package
text: sl_extract_waveforms() — expand GEDI L1B rxwaveform list columns long form interpolated elevation per sample, direct plotting downstream analysis

title: spacelaser: Fast Remote Reads of GEDI and ICESat-2 Lidar Data — spacelaser-package
text: sl_hdf5_groups() — list groups datasets inside remote HDF5 file sl_hdf5_read() — read single dataset full HDF5 path, returning typed R vector

title: spacelaser: Fast Remote Reads of GEDI and ICESat-2 Lidar Data — spacelaser-package
text: NASA Earthdata credentials required read hits DAAC endpoint. spacelaser resolves , order, EARTHDATA_USERNAME + EARTHDATA_PASSWORD environment variables .netrc file (via GDAL_HTTP_NETRC_FILE ~/.netrc). Register https://urs.earthdata.nasa.gov/ set earthdatalogin::edl_netrc() preferred.

title: spacelaser: Fast Remote Reads of GEDI and ICESat-2 Lidar Data — spacelaser-package
text: Maintainer: Hugh Graham hugh@belian.earth contributors: belian.earth [copyright holder]
